commands.repair_reason: The PR needs maintainer/contributor follow-up for the verify-path fix and real behavior proof; the missing proof gate should not be handled as an automated repair marker.; Needs attention: The PR intentionally changes scanner enforcement boundaries and still leaves one install/verify path consuming static malicious findings as blocking.; Review finding: [P1] Apply the static-as-context rule to verify — convex/httpAp... commands.review_summary:
The PR routes static scan findings into ClawScan context by removing static scan from direct public audit, package trust, moderation, autoban, and queue-priority enforcement paths, with related docs and tests.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: on the PR head, buildVerifySecurity still merges static malicious status into the verify security status. I did not run tests because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The PR needs maintainer/contributor follow-up for the verify-path fix and real behavior proof; the missing proof gate should not be handled as an automated repair marker.
